The '''past tense''' in Aelaki is the unmarked temporal frame, serving as the base form from which other tenses are derived. It refers to events that occurred before the current day, excluding [[Hesternal|yesterday]] (which uses the [[Hesternal|hesternal]]) and the deep legendary past (which uses the [[Mythic|mythic past]]).

== Formation ==

The past tense carries no dedicated tense marker. Instead, it is distinguished from the [[Present|present]] by the addition of evidential suffixes that indicate how the speaker learned about the event.

== Evidential suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Evidential !! Suffix !! Meaning
|-
| Auditory/Hearsay || '''-ro''' || The speaker heard the event or learned of it through oral report
|-
| Visual/Witnessed || '''-shë''' || The speaker personally saw or witnessed the event
|-
| Inferential || '''-shëm''' || The speaker infers the event from indirect evidence
|}

== Diachronic notes ==

The inferential suffix '''-shëm''' is historically a fusion of '''-shë''' (witnessed) and '''-mu''' (the [[Future|future]] inferential marker), suggesting that inference was originally conceptualized as "seeing toward" a conclusion.

== See also ==

* [[Mythic]] -- the deep legendary past
* [[Hesternal]] -- "yesterday"
* [[Present]]
* [[Future]]
* [[Hodiernal]]
* [[Crastinal]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
